name: Black Bean Soup#3
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a 10 to 12 quart pan over medium heat.
2. Add salt pork, ham, onions, garlic, and celery. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are very soft and lightly browned (about 40 minutes).
3. Rinse and sort the beans, then drain them. Add to the pan along with red pepper, cumin, and broth. Bring to a boil over high heat; reduce heat, cover, and simmer until beans easily mash (2 to 3 hours). If using ham hocks, remove and cool. Shred meat and return it to the pan; discard skin and bones.
4. Skim and discard any fat from the soup.
5. Blend portions of the soup in a blender or food processor until smooth. Cover and refrigerate if serving the next day.
6. Return the soup to the pot and heat until steaming, stirring often.
7. Add vinegar and sherry.
8. Serve with condiments arranged on the table for individual use:
- 3 lbs Warm Kielbasa, cut into half-inch slices
- 6 cups Hot Cooked Rice
- 8 Hard-Boiled Eggs, chopped
- 2 Cups Small Sweet Pickle Spears
- 2 Cups Diced Bell Pepper
